document.source_title: The War You’re Not Allowed to See: How the UAE Rewrites the Story of Iranian Strikes
document.summary: Bellingcat investigates how the UAE has downplayed the impact of Iranian drone strikes and mischaracterized interceptions. The UAE's government has issued warnings against sharing footage of these incidents, leading to arrests and a crackdown on information dissemination. Despite official narratives, evidence from social media and satellite imagery suggests a significant discrepancy between reported and actual events, raising concerns about the safety and transparency in the region.
key_takeaways: UAE authorities have downplayed the impact of Iranian drone strikes.
Legal actions have been taken against individuals sharing footage of attacks.
Satellite imagery contradicts official accounts of successful interceptions.
Influencers in the UAE are used to promote government narratives.
Public awareness of detentions has reduced the sharing of strike-related videos.
The UAE government has increased repression of independent reporting during the conflict.
document.why_it_matters: This investigation highlights the challenges of obtaining accurate information in conflict zones, particularly in the UAE, where government narratives may obscure the reality of ongoing threats. It raises critical questions about freedom of expression and the public's right to know during times of crisis.

claims.claim_text: The UAE has downplayed the damage from Iranian drone strikes.
claims.evidence_text: Bellingcat has identified several high-profile incidents where authorities in the United Arab Emirates have downplayed damage, mischaracterised interceptions and in some instances not acknowledged successful Iranian drone strikes on the country.

claims.claim_text: The UAE government has arrested individuals for sharing footage of drone strikes.
claims.evidence_text: The country’s attorney general has ordered the arrest of 35 people and said they would face an expedited trial for 'publishing video clips on social media platforms containing misleading, fabricated content.'

claims.claim_text: Satellite imagery contradicts official accounts of drone interceptions.
claims.evidence_text: Bellingcat found that some of the videos posted online as well as satellite imagery from the region contradicts a number of official accounts of high-profile attacks.

entities.name: Bellingcat
entities.description: An investigative journalism website that specializes in open-source intelligence.
entities.evidence_text: Bellingcat has identified several high-profile incidents where authorities in the United Arab Emirates have downplayed damage.

entities.name: UAE Government
entities.description: The governing body of the United Arab Emirates, responsible for national security and public information.
entities.evidence_text: The UAE’s attorney general warned that publication of images or videos of strikes was illegal.

entities.name: Dubai Media Office
entities.description: The press office of the Government of Dubai, responsible for public communications.
entities.evidence_text: The Dubai Media Office warned followers that legal action would be taken against those sharing 'unverified material.'

events.event: Coordinated attacks by the US and Israel on Iran.
events.evidence_text: Hours after the United States and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran on Feb. 28, the Islamic Republic responded by launching an attack against US-allies in the region including the UAE.

events.event: Fire at Jebel Ali Port attributed to debris from an aerial interception.
events.evidence_text: Authorities made a similar report on March 1, stating that a fire at one of the berths of Jebel Ali Port was caused by debris from an aerial interception.
On March 3, a video filmed from a vessel appears to show a drone striking the port of Fujairah.

events.event: Temporary suspension of operations at Dubai International Airport.
events.evidence_text: On March 7, the Dubai Media Office announced the temporary suspension of operations at Dubai International Airport.

metrics.name: Number of people arrested for sharing footage
metrics.evidence_text: The country’s attorney general has ordered the arrest of 35 people.

metrics.name: Number of people injured by strikes
metrics.evidence_text: As of April 1, UAE media reported that a total of 12 people had been killed and 190 injured by strikes since the beginning of the war.

citations.title: The War You’re Not Allowed to See: How the UAE Rewrites the Story of Iranian Strikes

source_credibility.reason: Bellingcat is known for its rigorous investigative journalism and use of open-source intelligence.

geolocation.name: Fujairah Port
geolocation.evidence_text: By March 7, satellite imagery shows at least three storage tanks fully destroyed.

geolocation.name: Jebel Ali Port
geolocation.evidence_text: Satellite imagery from the same day shows fires at two separate locations – approximately 3 km apart – within the port.

geolocation.name: Warda Complex
geolocation.evidence_text: On March 1, a drone struck a residential apartment on the 19th floor of the Warda complex in Dubai.

geolocation.name: Dubai International Airport
geolocation.evidence_text: Footage that emerged online around the same time shows a drone strike next to an airport terminal building.
